description: §8.3 real JOIN across two tables (RUNTIME, sqlEngine:"real"). booksByAuthor(name) runs `SELECT b.title, a.name FROM books b JOIN authors a ON a.id = b.author_id WHERE a.name = ${name} ORDER BY b.id`.all(); the real Bun.SQL engine joins books→authors, aliases the columns, and filters by the bound author name. The stub matches only the first FROM table (`books`) and returns its raw rows — it cannot join, alias, or reach the authors row — so the joined result passes ONLY on the real engine.
rationale: §8.3 `.all()` = Row[]. A JOIN is the canonical multi-table read the stub structurally cannot express (its regex extracts one `FROM <table>`). Fork B loose-infer: authors(id,name) + books(id,title,author_id) from the seed rows; no constraint under test so no <schema> is needed. The `${name}` bound param + the ON/WHERE/alias/ORDER BY are all real-engine work — empirically the stub returns the raw books rows (id/title/author_id), never {title, author}.
